title: Rollback partial Chronik JSONL appends under the ledger lock
goal: Harden the append-only Chronik storage primitive so a low-level short write or I/O failure after extending a ledger file either rolls back to the exact pre-append size under the same exclusive lock or returns a distinct fail-closed recovery condition without claiming a valid complete append.

acceptance.assertion: The storage primitive records the exact target size while holding the same cross-process exclusive lock used for append.
A simulated short write or append I/O failure truncates and fsyncs the ledger back to the exact pre-append size before the lock is released.
If rollback or directory/file durability cannot be established, the result is a distinct fail-closed recovery error and never a normal import success or ordinary duplicate result.

After every handled failure, a fresh byte-level snapshot either ends at the original complete JSONL boundary or reports explicit integrity failure; no decoder replacement or line filtering hides raw bytes.
Tests cover concurrent processes, target path replacement, lock ownership, short writes, ENOSPC, rollback success and rollback failure without weakening event-id conflict detection.
Chronik focused tests, full local validation, GitHub CI, versioned deploy and a live repeated import pass at the exact reviewed head.

rollback.trigger: The hardening truncates valid concurrent data, weakens append-only semantics or turns an uncertain rollback into success.
rollback.procedure: Restore the previous storage primitive and timer release, preserve all failure artifacts, and require manual ledger integrity verification before another import.

conditions: a foreign Chronik worktree, lease or process overlaps storage.py or its tests
the design cannot bind the pre-append size and rollback to the same lock and opened file identity
the change would rewrite previously valid ledger records rather than only undo the failed extension
raw-byte tail, concurrency or recovery tests fail

finding.scope: storage.py append durability after low-level partial write
finding.t006_result: T006 validates candidates and conflicts before append and fsyncs successful writes, but intentionally does not claim that an abrupt partial os.write or flush failure can never leave an incomplete tail.
finding.separate_existing_task: GRABOWSKI-OPERATOR-SURFACE-V1-T058 covers Grabowski audit-log append hardening, not the Chronik agent ledger, so it cannot close this scope.

metadata.priority_decision: Keep later than T007 because current ledger integrity is valid, incomplete tails fail closed on read/import, and no live corruption is observed; implement before treating abrupt append failures as fully recoverable.
does_not_establish: permission to repair or truncate the live ledger
that fsync alone rolls back a partial append
that Grabowski T058 fixes Chronik storage
